active_conflicts: Iraqi PMF vs US bases in Iraq
negotiation_status: COLLAPSED - Iran rejected Trump's claim of talks and 5-day ceasefire. No active negotiations.

key_events_timeline.event: Operation Epic Fury launched - 900 strikes in 12 hours
Ayatollah Ali Khamenei assassinated in Israeli airstrike
Iran retaliates - 500+ ballistic missiles, 2000+ drones
Hezbollah opens Lebanon front
Mojtaba Khamenei installed as new Supreme Leader by IRGC
Iran announces 3 demands to end war
Israel warns US: critically low on interceptors
Israel kills Ali Larijani, IRGC Commander Pakpour, Defense Minister
Israel strikes Iran Asaluyeh complex (South Pars gas field)
Iran retaliates: destroys Qatar Ras Laffan LNG (17% capacity), hits Kuwait/Saudi/UAE energy
Brent crude hits $119
Houthis declare 'all military options possible'
Trump announces 5-day pause on energy strikes via Truth Social
BREAKING: Iran REJECTS Trump's claim of talks and 5-day ceasefire. Denies any negotiations are taking place. No pause acknowledged.

Iran strikes towns near Israel's Dimona nuclear facility — 180 wounded (116 in Arad, 64 in Dimona). First time Iranian missiles penetrate Israeli air defenses near nuclear site. IAEA confirms no damage to reactor, no abnormal radiation.

Iran launches 2 intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia (US-UK base, Indian Ocean, 2000+ miles from Tehran). Missiles did not hit but demonstrated range capability reaching Western European capitals (Paris, Berlin, Rome). Iran denies, calls it 'false flag'.
Israel's Chief of Staff confirms Iran's missiles can reach European capitals. Sejjil-3 development (4,000km range) under testing.
Netanyahu claims Iran no longer has uranium enrichment capacity after strikes on nuclear sites.

China's MizarVision satellite company publicly releasing high-res imagery of US military positions (F-22s at Prince Sultan Air Base, carrier groups). US accuses China of enabling Iranian attacks via commercial satellite intelligence. China denies.
Al Jazeera reports Iran may be using China's BeiDou navigation system for missile guidance, bypassing GPS.
Iranian drone swarm damages US fighter jet at Al Udeid Air Base — heat-seeking drones evaded base defenses.
Iran strikes secret Israeli forward command center — 7 senior IDF officials killed including 2 brigadier generals. Intelligence breach suspected.
David's Sling interceptor stocks exhausted. Patriot batteries down to last reserve. Arrow-3 at <30% capacity. Israel entering critical defense gap.

Iran formally demands reparations for war damages as precondition for any ceasefire negotiations. Demands include compensation for civilian casualties, infrastructure destruction, and nuclear facility damage.

iran_sejjil_dancing_missile.feature: High-altitude maneuvering ('dancing') evades missile defense systems — filmed evading Israeli interceptors in combat
iran_sejjil_dancing_missile.combat_proven: Successfully penetrated Israeli air defenses near Dimona nuclear facility (March 21)

iran_cluster_munitions.description: Added to ballistic missile warheads, forces multiple interceptor engagements per missile, overwhelming defense systems

cost_asymmetry.description: Iran's $50,000 drones and cheap missiles are being shot down by Israel's $1-3 million interceptor missiles
cost_asymmetry.ratio: Iran spends $1 for every $20-60 Israel spends defending — economically unsustainable for Israel
cost_asymmetry.result: Israel cannot maintain interceptor production fast enough to replace stocks

israel_interceptor_crisis.iron_dome_status: depleted (focused on Lebanon front vs Hezbollah rockets)
israel_interceptor_crisis.production_ramp: Deal signed Dec 2025 but new interceptors take years to manufacture
israel_interceptor_crisis.asymmetry: Each Iranian salvo costs Israel 20-60x more to defend than Iran spends attacking
israel_interceptor_crisis.defense_gap: Israel entering period with no viable missile defense against Iranian ballistic missiles

us_aircraft_damage.incident: Iranian heat-seeking drone swarm damaged US fighter jet at Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar (March 23)
us_aircraft_damage.significance: First successful Iranian strike on US aircraft, demonstrated ability to reach US bases in Gulf

israeli_command_center_strike.incident: Iran struck secret Israeli forward command center on March 24
israeli_command_center_strike.casualties: 7 senior IDF officials killed including 2 brigadier generals
israeli_command_center_strike.significance: Intelligence breach — Iran knew location of classified facility. Possible HUMINT or satellite intelligence from China
israeli_command_center_strike.impact: Major blow to Israeli command structure and morale

energy_infrastructure_damage.iran_asaluyeh: 4 gas treatment plants damaged by Israeli drone strike (South Pars)

iran_3_demands.demand_1: Recognition of Iran's legitimate rights including nuclear program — Iran insists its enrichment activities are legal under NPT
iran_3_demands.demand_2: Payment of reparations for war damages — compensation for 12,000+ civilian casualties, destroyed nuclear facilities, refineries, infrastructure. Iran has not publicly named a specific dollar figure but damage estimates exceed $50 billion
iran_3_demands.demand_3: Firm international guarantees against future aggression — must be backed by UNSC resolution, not just US promises (which Iran considers worthless after JCPOA withdrawal)

additional_demands.us_bases_withdrawal: Iran demands departure of ALL US military bases from GCC states — Al Udeid (Qatar), Al Dhafra (UAE), Camp Arifjan (Kuwait), Prince Sultan Air Base (Saudi Arabia), Bahrain 5th Fleet HQ. Iran views US military presence as occupation enabling aggression.
additional_demands.sanctions_lifted: Complete lifting of all US sanctions including on Iran's central bank (CBI), oil exports, SWIFT access. Iran sees sanctions as economic warfare that preceded military warfare.
additional_demands.central_bank_unfrozen: Unfreezing of $100+ billion in Iranian assets frozen in foreign banks. Iran views US control of global financial system (SWIFT, dollar clearing) as weaponized infrastructure.
additional_demands.no_regime_change_guarantee: Explicit guarantee that US/Israel will not pursue regime change in Iran — to be enshrined in international law
additional_demands.nuclear_rights_recognition: International recognition of Iran's right to full nuclear fuel cycle including enrichment to any level for peaceful purposes

iran_3_demands.ceasefire_position: Iran rejects temporary ceasefire — demands permanent end to war with guarantees. Reparations and US base withdrawal are non-negotiable preconditions for any talks. Hormuz stays closed until demands are met.
iran_3_demands.iran_leverage: Iran holds: Hormuz closure (21M bpd), submarine cable threat (20% global internet), GCC desalination vulnerability (15-20M people at risk), continued missile capability, proxy network activation, and the demonstrated ability to strike Israeli command centers and US bases.

likelihood_of_acceptance.reparations: VERY LOW — no precedent for attacking side paying reparations while claiming victory. US paid nothing after Iraq War despite WMD being fabricated.
likelihood_of_acceptance.us_bases_withdrawal: EXTREMELY LOW — would end US regional hegemony. US has never voluntarily withdrawn from strategic region under military pressure. Withdrawal would signal end of American military superiority in Middle East.
likelihood_of_acceptance.sanctions_lifted: MODERATE — sanctions relief was traded before (JCPOA 2015). Most negotiable demand. But current US political climate makes it difficult.
likelihood_of_acceptance.overall_assessment: Iran's maximalist demands are likely opening positions for negotiation. Historical precedent (Iran-Iraq War 1988) shows Iran eventually accepts less. But Mojtaba Khamenei's IRGC-aligned leadership may have higher threshold than his father.

us_hegemony_challenged.description: This war demonstrates limits of US military superiority — air power alone cannot defeat Iran, and Iran's asymmetric capabilities (cheap drones vs expensive interceptors) have exposed a structural vulnerability
us_hegemony_challenged.cost_asymmetry_proven: Iran's $50K drones destroyed by $3M interceptors proves conventional military dominance can be negated by mass-produced cheap weapons
us_hegemony_challenged.ground_invasion_impossible: US 2003 DoD study: 500K troops, 10-20K US casualties for Iran ground invasion — politically impossible in any scenario
us_hegemony_challenged.carrier_vulnerability: Iranian anti-ship missiles and drone swarms threaten US carrier groups, challenging decades of naval power projection doctrine

petrodollar_implications.description: Iran's demand to free its central bank from US sanctions challenges the US-controlled global financial architecture
petrodollar_implications.swift_weaponization: US used SWIFT access as a weapon against Iran — other nations (China, Russia, India) are developing alternatives (CIPS, SPFS, UPI)
petrodollar_implications.dollar_reserve_erosion: War accelerates de-dollarization trend. China and Saudi Arabia already conduct some oil trade in yuan. War may push GCC states to diversify away from dollar dependency.
petrodollar_implications.cbdc_implications: US push for CBDC/tokenized finance could enable even more financial control. Iran's resistance is partly about preventing a future where US monitors/controls all global transactions.

israel_sabotaging_peace.description: Israel has strategic interest in continuing the war to permanently degrade Iran's nuclear capability. Any ceasefire risks Iran rebuilding.
israel_sabotaging_peace.evidence: Israel assassinated Khamenei on Day 1, killed Ali Larijani and IRGC commanders, struck nuclear facilities — all actions designed to make negotiation impossible
israel_sabotaging_peace.netanyahu_calculation: Netanyahu's political survival depends on continued conflict. Peace would bring investigations, accountability. War provides political cover.
israel_sabotaging_peace.historical_pattern: Israel has historically opposed US-Iran diplomatic engagement (opposed JCPOA, lobbied against Obama's outreach)

china_russia_alignment.description: War is accelerating a China-Russia-Iran strategic alignment against US hegemony
china_russia_alignment.china_role: Satellite intelligence (MizarVision), possible BeiDou missile guidance, diplomatic cover at UNSC
china_russia_alignment.russia_role: S-300 air defense delivered pre-war, S-400 negotiations ongoing. Su-35 fighter jets deal signed. Mi-28 attack helicopters. Russia-Iran quid pro quo: Iran supplied Shahed drones for Ukraine, Russia transferring military technology in return. UNSC veto protects Iran diplomatically. Reports (unconfirmed) of covert munitions resupply during the war via Caspian Sea route.
china_russia_alignment.russia_iran_arms_confirmed: S-300 (delivered 2016), Su-35 deal (2023-24), Yak-130 trainers, technology transfers in exchange for Shahed drones used in Ukraine. Russia has historically been Iran's primary advanced weapons supplier.
china_russia_alignment.china_iran_arms_confirmed: Chinese commercial components confirmed in Iranian Shahed drones (engines, microcontrollers, navigation modules — found in Ukrainian teardowns). Chinese firms sanctioned by US for missile technology transfers to Iran. Iran's drone production relies on Chinese dual-use component supply chain. MizarVision satellite imagery openly shared. BeiDou navigation system possibly integrated into Iranian missiles.
china_russia_alignment.north_korea_iran_confirmed: Iran's Shahab-3 missile derived from North Korea's Nodong. Ongoing collaboration on ballistic missile technology confirmed by UN Panel of Experts. Component and knowledge sharing continues.
china_russia_alignment.covert_resupply_assessment: During active war, Russia and China have strong incentive to resupply Iran covertly — weakening US military credibility benefits both. Caspian Sea route (Russia) and overland via Central Asia (China) are difficult to interdict. If confirmed, this would mean Iran's missile/drone stockpile is being replenished, extending the war indefinitely.
china_russia_alignment.multipolar_shift: This war may be remembered as the moment US unipolar dominance ended — similar to Suez 1956 ending British Empire

us_israel_advantages.description: Factors favoring US/Israel coalition that must be weighed against Iran's asymmetric advantages

air_superiority.status: Total and unchallenged — US/Israel control the airspace over the entire theater
air_superiority.assets: F-35s (stealth, Iran cannot detect), F-22s, B-2 bombers, carrier-based F/A-18s, aerial refueling fleet
air_superiority.iran_has_no_answer: Iran has NO modern fighter aircraft capable of contesting US/Israeli air superiority. Iran's air force flies 1970s-era F-14s and F-4s.
air_superiority.implication: US/Israel can strike ANY target in Iran at will. Iran cannot prevent this. Every Iranian military facility, leadership compound, and infrastructure node is vulnerable from the air.

intelligence_superiority.five_eyes: US has Five Eyes intelligence network (US, UK, Australia, Canada, New Zealand) providing signals intelligence globally
intelligence_superiority.satellite_constellation: US operates 100+ military/intelligence satellites vs Iran's limited capabilities. Real-time surveillance of every Iranian military movement.
intelligence_superiority.cyber_capability: US/Israel are top-tier cyber powers (Stuxnet precedent). Can disrupt Iranian command/control, communications, power grid.
intelligence_superiority.humint: Mossad has deep penetration of Iranian military/political establishment (demonstrated by assassinations of nuclear scientists 2010-2020)
intelligence_superiority.iran_intelligence_gap: Iran relies on Chinese satellite imagery and has limited organic ISR capability

nuclear_escalation_deterrent.israel_nuclear_arsenal: Israel has estimated 80-90 nuclear warheads (undeclared). This is the ultimate deterrent — Iran knows any existential threat to Israel risks nuclear response.
nuclear_escalation_deterrent.us_nuclear_umbrella: US extended deterrence covers Gulf allies. Nuclear escalation is theoretically possible if Iran crosses certain thresholds.
nuclear_escalation_deterrent.iran_has_no_nuclear_weapons: Iran does NOT have nuclear weapons. Despite breakout capability, there is no assembled weapon. This is a fundamental asymmetry.
nuclear_escalation_deterrent.constraint_on_iran: Iran cannot push for total military victory because Israel's nuclear arsenal means any existential threat triggers potential nuclear response

economic_warfare_capability.dollar_dominance: US controls the global reserve currency. Can freeze any country's assets, cut off SWIFT access, impose secondary sanctions on anyone trading with Iran.
economic_warfare_capability.sanctions_impact_on_iran: Iran's GDP contracted ~6% under maximum pressure sanctions. Oil exports dropped from 2.5M bpd to <500K bpd. Currency lost 80% of value.
economic_warfare_capability.iran_economic_weakness: Iran's economy was already struggling pre-war: 40%+ inflation, 10%+ unemployment, brain drain, infrastructure decay from decades of sanctions
economic_warfare_capability.oil_weapon_cuts_both_ways: While Hormuz closure hurts global markets, it also completely cuts off Iran's own oil export revenue — Iran earns $0 while Hormuz is closed

military_industrial_capacity.us_defense_budget: $886 billion (2024) — more than the next 10 countries combined. Iran's defense budget: ~$10 billion.
military_industrial_capacity.production_capacity: US can ramp up munitions production. Raytheon, Lockheed, Northrop Grumman have massive capacity. The constraint is time, not capability.
military_industrial_capacity.stockpile_depth: US has strategic reserves of precision munitions, cruise missiles (Tomahawk inventory: 4,000+), and JDAMs
military_industrial_capacity.replenishment: While Israel's interceptors are depleting, US can surge-deliver Patriot interceptors and is ramping THAAD deployment

coalition_and_alliances.nato_article_5: Attack on US bases could trigger NATO Article 5 consultation (though not automatic invocation for out-of-area operations)
coalition_and_alliances.gcc_basing: US has forces in Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia — massive forward-deployed capability
coalition_and_alliances.israel_qualitative_military_edge: US law requires maintaining Israel's qualitative military edge (QME) over all regional adversaries
coalition_and_alliances.diplomatic_weight: US has UNSC veto power, controls IMF/World Bank, can rally G7 support

iran_military_weaknesses.air_force: Iran's air force is 1970s-era (F-14A Tomcats, F-4 Phantoms, F-5 Tigers). Cannot contest airspace.
iran_military_weaknesses.navy: Iran's navy is coastal — fast boats and midget submarines. No blue-water capability. US Navy dominates open ocean.
iran_military_weaknesses.precision_munitions: While Iran has improved accuracy, most Iranian missiles are still less precise than US/Israeli precision-guided munitions
iran_military_weaknesses.command_and_control: Iranian C2 is vulnerable to US cyber and kinetic strikes. Multiple senior commanders already killed.
iran_military_weaknesses.economic_fragility: Iran cannot sustain prolonged conflict economically — GDP $400B vs US $28T. Sanctions already devastating.
iran_military_weaknesses.domestic_discontent: Iranian population has shown willingness to protest (2019, 2022 Mahsa Amini protests). Prolonged war with 12,000+ civilian casualties could trigger internal unrest.
iran_military_weaknesses.brain_drain: Iran has lost millions of educated citizens to emigration. Technical talent pool is shrinking.

damage_already_inflicted_on_iran.nuclear_program: Natanz heavily damaged. Multiple enrichment facilities struck. Even if Fordow survived, overall program significantly set back 2-5 years.
damage_already_inflicted_on_iran.military_infrastructure: IRGC bases, missile production facilities, radar installations struck extensively in Day 1 (900 strikes in 12 hours)
damage_already_inflicted_on_iran.economic_damage: Asaluyeh (South Pars) gas complex damaged — Iran's primary revenue source. Isfahan and Abadan refineries struck.
damage_already_inflicted_on_iran.leadership_losses: Supreme Leader Khamenei assassinated Day 1. Ali Larijani killed. IRGC Commander Pakpour killed. Defense Minister killed. Massive leadership disruption.
damage_already_inflicted_on_iran.casualties: 5,300 military + 12,000 civilian = 17,300 total Iranian casualties in 26 days. This is devastating even for a country with high casualty tolerance.

historical_precedent_favoring_us.desert_storm_1991: US achieved total air dominance and destroyed Iraqi military (4th largest in world) in 42 days. Iran's military is not as large.
historical_precedent_favoring_us.us_has_never_lost_an_air_campaign: The US has never been defeated in a conflict where it maintained air superiority. The question is whether air superiority alone achieves political objectives.
historical_precedent_favoring_us.sanctions_pressure_works_over_time: Maximum pressure sanctions 2018-2021 reduced Iran's oil exports by 80% and caused severe economic contraction

nuclear_status.iran_breakout_time_pre_war: Less than 1 week for enough HEU for one bomb (DIA assessment May 2025)
nuclear_status.iran_enriched_uranium_stockpile: Enough 60% enriched uranium for 12+ weapons if further enriched (ODNI Nov 2024)
nuclear_status.iran_enrichment_capacity_post_strikes: Netanyahu claims destroyed. IAEA says material 'probably still there'. Natanz struck but underground facilities may survive.
nuclear_status.dimona_strike_result: 180 wounded near Dimona. IAEA confirms no reactor damage, no radiation. But demonstrates Iran CAN target Israeli nuclear sites.
nuclear_status.iran_nuclear_weapon_status: Pre-war: could assemble a device in 6-12 months if decision made. Post-strikes: enrichment capacity degraded but not eliminated. Underground facilities may be intact.

iran_missile_drone_stockpile.fired_so_far: 500+ ballistic missiles, 2000+ drones (as of Mar 5, CENTCOM)
iran_missile_drone_stockpile.remaining_capacity: Analysts estimate 2-3 months of sustained operations
iran_missile_drone_stockpile.production_rate_missiles: Ongoing but slower than drone production. Dual-use facilities can be retooled.
iran_missile_drone_stockpile.iran_official_claim: IRGC says stockpile NOT depleted, production continues

iran_extended_range_capability.diego_garcia_strike: 2 IRBMs launched at Diego Garcia (3,500km from Iran). Did not hit but demonstrated range. Iran denies, claims false flag.
iran_extended_range_capability.european_range: Sejjil-2 (2,400km) can reach southeastern Europe. Sejjil-3 under development (4,000km) would reach Paris, Berlin, Rome.
iran_extended_range_capability.israel_chief_of_staff_statement: Iran's missiles can reach European capitals
iran_extended_range_capability.implication: Iran's deterrence now extends beyond Middle East to NATO territory

china_intelligence_support.satellite_imagery: Chinese company MizarVision publicly releasing high-res satellite imagery of US military positions (F-22s at Prince Sultan Air Base, carrier groups). Sub-1-meter resolution.
china_intelligence_support.jilin_1_constellation: China's Jilin-1 satellite network provides near-continuous surveillance of US operations in Middle East
china_intelligence_support.beidou_navigation: Reports suggest Iran may be using China's BeiDou navigation system for missile guidance, bypassing GPS
china_intelligence_support.us_accusation: Deputy NSA Jon Finer accused China of enabling Iranian attacks via commercial satellite intel. China denies.
china_intelligence_support.intelligence_value: Real-time tracking of US military assets allows Iran to time attacks and avoid air patrols

israel_air_defense_collapse.dimona_penetration: First time Iranian missiles penetrated air defenses near nuclear facility (March 21)
israel_air_defense_collapse.interceptor_status: Arrow 3, David's Sling, Iron Dome all critically depleted. IDF spokesman admitted failure to intercept 'not special or unfamiliar' missiles.
israel_air_defense_collapse.asymmetry_worsening: Iran's cheap missiles vs Israel's expensive interceptors — ratio now catastrophic. Each salvo degrades defense further.
israel_air_defense_collapse.production_gap: New interceptor production deal signed Dec 2025 but takes years to deliver. Israel entering defense gap.

energy_infrastructure.saudi_aramco_damage: Abqaiq processing facility struck, Ras Tanura terminal damaged
energy_infrastructure.qatar_lng: North Field operations reduced 40% due to shipping insurance costs
energy_infrastructure.uae_ports: Jebel Ali port insurance premiums 10x, some shippers rerouting
energy_infrastructure.kuwait_oil: Mina al-Ahmadi storage hit by drone strike
energy_infrastructure.iran_refineries: Abadan, Isfahan refineries struck by US/Israel
energy_infrastructure.hormuz_status: Closed — Iran mining + IRGC fast boats

humanitarian_crisis.gcc_expat_exodus: Estimated 2 million expats have left UAE/Qatar/Kuwait since war began
humanitarian_crisis.gcc_desalination_threat: Iran has explicitly threatened desalination plants
humanitarian_crisis.global_food_price_impact: Wheat +40%, shipping costs +300% through Suez alternative routes

submarine_cables.description: 14+ submarine fiber optic cables transit the Strait of Hormuz and Persian Gulf, carrying approximately 17-25% of global internet traffic
submarine_cables.vulnerability: Cables at 50-100m depth in Hormuz narrows, within range of Iranian submarines (Fateh-class, Ghadir-class) and sea mines
submarine_cables.iran_capability: Iran has Ghadir-class midget submarines, mine-laying capability, and IRGC Navy divers trained for underwater sabotage
submarine_cables.economic_impact_if_severed: Estimated $1.5-4 billion per day in disrupted digital commerce; banking, oil trading, logistics communications severely impacted
submarine_cables.redundancy: Limited — most Gulf-connected cables converge at Fujairah, creating a single point of failure

gcc_water_vulnerability.description: GCC states depend on desalination for 60-99% of potable water, with only 2-5 days of reserves

qatar.natural_water_ratio: Consumes approximately 17,000x its naturally occurring renewable freshwater

kuwait.natural_water_ratio: Consumes approximately 12,000x its naturally occurring renewable freshwater

uae.natural_water_ratio: Consumes approximately 8,000x its naturally occurring renewable freshwater

major_plants: Ras Al Khair (1,025,000 m3/day - largest in world)
saudi_arabia.east_coast_vulnerability: Jubail and Ras Al Khair plants are within Iranian missile range (300km from Iranian coast)

gcc_water_vulnerability.scenario_impact: If Iran strikes 3-4 major desalination plants, 15-20 million people face water crisis within 72 hours

gcc_food_dependency.description: GCC states import 80-95% of food, primarily through maritime routes vulnerable to disruption
gcc_food_dependency.if_hormuz_closed: 60% of food imports disrupted; wheat prices surge 40-80%; rice shortages within 2-4 weeks

iran_geography.description: Iran's geography makes ground invasion extremely difficult — no modern army has successfully invaded Iran
iran_geography.comparison: 3.5x the size of Iraq, larger than France+Germany+UK combined

zagros_mountains.description: 1,600km mountain chain along western border, average elevation 3,000-4,000m
zagros_mountains.military_significance: Natural fortress — any invasion from Iraq/Turkey must cross these mountains through limited passes
zagros_mountains.passes: Only 4-5 usable mountain passes for heavy armor, all pre-sighted by Iranian artillery

elburz_mountains.description: Separates Tehran from Caspian coast, peaks to 5,671m (Mt Damavand)

central_plateau.description: Vast desert interior (Dasht-e Kavir, Dasht-e Lut) — uninhabitable salt desert
central_plateau.military_significance: No concealment, extreme heat (ground temp 80°C in Lut), impossible logistics

underground_facilities.fordow: Built 80m under mountain near Qom, survived Israeli/US strikes
underground_facilities.natanz: Partially underground, heavily damaged but rebuilt deeper
underground_facilities.imam_ali_missile_base: Network of tunnels in Zagros mountains, houses medium-range ballistic missiles
underground_facilities.underground_cities: Multiple underground weapons factories and command centers mapped by satellite but unreachable by conventional bunker busters

iran_geography.historical_context: No foreign army has occupied Tehran since the Anglo-Soviet invasion of 1941, and that required a two-front surprise attack while Iran was neutral. The US military estimated 500,000+ troops and 18-24 months for a ground invasion in 2003 planning studies.
iran_geography.us_military_assessment: 2003 DoD planning studies concluded ground invasion of Iran would require 500,000+ troops minimum, 18-24 month campaign, with estimated 10,000-20,000 US casualties, making it politically impossible

houthi_capability.assessment: Houthis have demonstrated sustained ability to threaten Red Sea shipping despite US/UK Operation Prosperity Guardian

hezbollah_naval.anti_ship_missiles: C-802 (used successfully against INS Hanit in 2006)
hezbollah_naval.assessment: Limited naval capability but could threaten Eastern Mediterranean shipping

economic_impact.2024_disruption_impact: 30-40% drop in Suez traffic, $200B+ annual cost to global economy
